description: Create a comprehensive lead capture form system with 5 different form types, each placed at optimal locations across the website. Each form type has distinct HTML field IDs for CRM mapping in Apollo.
steps: Create a new branch 'feat/lead-capture-forms' from current HEAD (feat/apollo-tracker)

Create components/lead-capture/NewsletterInlineForm.tsx - A beautiful inline newsletter signup form with gradient background, email + optional name fields. Uses form IDs: 'costsmart-newsletter-email', 'costsmart-newsletter-name'. Place after blog highlights section on homepage (app/page.tsx). Design: dark navy card with emerald accent, subtle glow effect.

Create components/lead-capture/ExitIntentPopup.tsx - A modal that triggers when user moves mouse toward browser top (exit intent detection). Offers a free 'Financial Planning Toolkit' PDF as lead magnet. Fields: 'costsmart-exit-email', 'costsmart-exit-name'. Design: clean white modal with emerald gradient header, overlay backdrop. Includes dismiss logic with localStorage to not re-show for 7 days.

Create components/lead-capture/FloatingBottomBar.tsx - A non-intrusive bottom bar that slides up after user scrolls 60% of the page. Offers 'weekly financial tips'. Field: 'costsmart-bottombar-email'. Design: slim bar with frosted glass effect, emerald CTA button. Includes close button and localStorage dismiss for session.

Create components/lead-capture/CalculatorResultGate.tsx - An inline CTA shown below calculator results, offering to email detailed calculation reports. Fields: 'costsmart-calcgate-email', 'costsmart-calcgate-name'. Design: bordered card matching the emerald/teal theme with a 'Get Full Report' value proposition.

Create components/lead-capture/BlogSidebarForm.tsx - A sticky sidebar widget for blog article pages offering 'Get Smart Money Insights' weekly digest. Field: 'costsmart-sidebar-email'. Design: compact card with soft gradient border, emerald accent.
Add ExitIntentPopup and FloatingBottomBar to app/layout.tsx (global, shown on all pages)
Add NewsletterInlineForm to app/page.tsx between the blog highlights section and the trust section
Add a CalculatorResultGate import note in a comment at the calculator page (app/in/sip-calculator/page.tsx) as an example placement, and actually add the component there below the AfterResultAd
Add BlogSidebarForm to one example blog post (app/blog/sip-vs-lumpsum/page.tsx) as a demonstration of blog integration
Create a reference document at LEAD_CAPTURE_FIELD_IDS.md in the project root listing all form types, their HTML field identifiers, form names, and placement locations for CRM mapping
acceptance_criteria: 5 distinct lead capture form components exist in components/lead-capture/
Each form has unique, descriptive HTML id/name attributes on input fields for CRM field mapping
ExitIntentPopup detects exit intent (mouseout near top of viewport) and shows a modal
FloatingBottomBar appears after 60% scroll and can be dismissed
All forms submit data (console.log for now since no backend) and show success state
All forms match the existing dark navy + emerald/teal design aesthetic
LEAD_CAPTURE_FIELD_IDS.md documents every form with its HTML identifiers
npm run build succeeds with no errors
verification: Run 'npm run build' and confirm it completes without errors
Verify all 5 components exist in components/lead-capture/
Verify LEAD_CAPTURE_FIELD_IDS.md exists and lists all form fields
findings: All 5 components built and integrated successfully. Build passes cleanly. Components are client-side only ('use client'). The blog post page was restructured to include a sidebar grid layout for the BlogSidebarForm. All form submissions log to console. ExitIntentPopup uses localStorage (7-day dismiss), FloatingBottomBar uses sessionStorage (session dismiss). Post-review fixes: Added cross-form submission memory via shared 'costsmart-lead-captured' localStorage key (all forms check before rendering and set on submit). Added LeadCaptureContext provider to coordinate ExitIntentPopup and FloatingBottomBar visibility (bottom bar hides when exit popup is active). Fixed blog article CTA block to use flex-col sm:flex-row for narrower grid column and replaced min-w-full with w-full on the comparison table. The preventDefault/Apollo issue was deemed acceptable since Apollo's modern tracker uses MutationObserver and field value capturing.
